name: CLR
description: Continuous Linear System Representation - Transfer function block with polynomial coefficients
xmlExample:
<BasicBlock id="b1" parent="0:2:0" interfaceFunctionName="CLR" blockType="c" dependsOnU="0" dependsOnT="1" simulationFunctionName="csslti4" simulationFunctionType="C_OR_FORTRAN" style="CLR">
  <ScilabString as="exprs" height="2" width="1">
    <data line="0" column="0" value="1"/>
    <data line="1" column="0" value="1+s"/>
  </ScilabString>
  <ScilabDouble as="realParameters" height="1" width="4">
    <data line="0" column="0" realPart="-1.0"/>
    <data line="0" column="1" realPart="1.0"/>
    <data line="0" column="2" realPart="1.0"/>
    <data line="0" column="3" realPart="0.0"/>
  </ScilabDouble>
  <mxGeometry as="geometry" x="0.0" y="-10.0" width="60.0" height="60.0"/>
</BasicBlock>
anomalies: rotation attribute included in port style
dependsOnT=1 (time-dependent) but dependsOnU=0 (not input-dependent) - uses internal state
criticalRules: Time-dependent but not directly input-dependent (internal dynamics)
commonUses: Implementing linear filters and transfer functions
Modeling first and second order systems
Signal conditioning with known frequency response
